channel.title: Sqvrltastic's Homepage
channel.description: Hi, I'm sqvrltastic and I'm a digital artist. Welcome to my website! This is my main place for posting my artwork and sharing my thoughts.

item.title: Website update
item.description: I rewrote and changed a few pages a bit. Mainly the landing page and the "about this website" page (to make it less ranty and more informative... LMAO 🥀). I also added some new titles in the favorite videogames section and added some new stamps (most of which I made! mainly the SoB and Lamenter ones) to the "about me" page, I think they're very cute and I just love hoarding graphics lol... 💖 🫠 Also, I added a couple new links to the coding and art resources sections.

item.title: Deep Rock Galactic female gunner concept [artwork]
A guide to self-hosting and optimizing your website's fonts (+ some typography tips) [guide]
item.description: Learn how and why you should self-host your website's fonts and the various methods to do so. Very in-depth!

item.title: OC: Luca von Valancious character sheet [artwork]
Deshittify your browsing experience [guide]
item.description: In recent years, there has been a surge of interest in topics such as digital minimalism and privacy in the digital era. Countless guides on topics such as reducing your digital footprint, debloating your OS and curbing your social media addiction have been written, some good, some mediocre and I'm about to add my own one to the growing pile...

item.title: Website update
item.description: Added a few new links into the "Coding resources" section.

item.title: Website update
item.description: I added a new page in the "Resources" section named "Miscellaneous useful links". I moved the content from the "Not indie web related" section ("Other indie websites" page) there, as well one new link! Also, happy Valentine's Day! 💞

item.title: Website update
item.description: I refactored the code for the dark theme and mobile version and (hopefully) fixed a few minor glitches. I also made the code overall more optimized, but you probably won't notice much difference as an end user.

item.title: "Free" web hosting services are not free [journal]
item.description: Lately I've been witnessing how Neocities is slowly falling apart, with Kyle only occasionally chiming in to solve only the most egregious and pressing technical issues and I have some thoughts on the matter...

item.title: Website update
item.description: Version 3.0 of the website is live! 🎉 I pretty much changed the entire website and all its sections. Also, I updated the "Animating details HTML element with pure CSS (without calc-size(auto))" guide and the "100 Questions for Webmasters" journal post and yes it's now called Journal and not Blog. And I made a new 88x31 button. PLEASE UPDATE ANY BACKLINKS THANKS and sorry for the inconvenience!

item.title: Animating details HTML element with pure CSS (without calc-size(auto)) [guide]
item.description: I needed some ingenious solution to pretty-up my details element styling. This guide is a temporary workaround until the calc-size() CSS function becomes widely available.

item.title: Website update
item.description: I finally added a guestbook! Honestly, it took me so long because I am very particular regarding the features I am looking for in such a service. I also hate relying on 3rd party services, but I am too lazy to figure out how to code my own solution. Self-hosting my own guestbooks is still something I am considering doing at some point, so consider this Atabook-powered guestbook a temporary solution. I will probably move web hosts by that point though.

item.title: Website update
item.description: Lots of improvements to general usability, optimization and aesthetics of the site. Including: better details element styling, much better web font optimization, converted every artwork to .avif format and further compressed them and included a link to download a full (.jpg) version under each one, added a lot of new coding resources in the links section. Check it out! 🔥 And more! But it's mostly just code optimization.

item.title: 100 Questions for Webmasters [journal]
item.description: A fun questionaire I found, made by mouseling. I was itching to write something lately and this is a perfect way to warm up my writing muscles. Warning for brutal honesty ahead.

item.title: Censorship is bad, actually [journal]
item.description: I usually prefer to avoid talking about hot-button political issues and controversial topics, but in light of recent events I decided to share my thoughts on the matter because it's closely related to my work and art as a whole.

item.title: Montenegro-chan offering you a cold one [artwork]
